description: Located within Yuexiu Park, Zhenhai Tower showcases over 2,000 years of Guangzhou's urban development history.
Take a boat tour along the Pearl River to enjoy the night views of both banks, featuring Guangzhou's modern cityscape and historic architecture.
A large commemorative building dedicated to Dr. Sun Yat-sen, an architectural masterpiece of modern China.
A typical Xiguan mansion, showcasing traditional Xiguan folk culture and lifestyle.
A renowned shopping center in Guangzhou, featuring numerous brands and gourmet options for an excellent shopping experience.
The largest comprehensive museum in South China, housing Guangdong's historical artifacts and natural specimens.
A large urban square located on Guangzhou's new central axis, serving as the core scenic area of the CBD.
Guangzhou's most distinctive arcade-style commercial street, a hub for traditional Cantonese cuisine.

attraction_name: Museum of the Nanyue King (Palace Exhibition Area)
description: Showcasing the archaeological site of the Nanyue Kingdom Palace from 2,000 years ago, an important testament to Guangzhou's city-building history.

attraction_name: Guangdong Museum of Art (Ersha Island Branch)
description: A significant art exhibition institution in South China, regularly hosting contemporary art exhibitions.
A Qing Dynasty architectural gem of Lingnan style, home to the Guangdong Folk Art Museum.
A large integrated shopping and entertainment center with multiple restaurants, cinemas, and entertainment facilities.

attraction_name: Chen Clan Ancestral Hall (Guangdong Folk Art Museum)
description: A representative work of Qing Dynasty Lingnan architectural art, combining wood carvings, brick carvings, and ceramic sculptures.
A cluster of Republic-era villas, featuring artistic cafes and bookstores, exuding a strong bohemian charm.
An underground archaeological site on Beijing Road, showcasing the historical evolution of Guangzhou's thousand-year-old commercial district.
